reasoning: Based on comprehensive data up to the October 26, 2025 knowledge cutoff, the probability of a 25 bps rate cut at the October 28-29 FOMC meeting is overwhelmingly high. A Reuters poll published on October 21 shows 117 out of 117 economists predicting a 25 bps cut. This is corroborated by the CME FedWatch tool, which on October 24 indicated an approximate 99% probability for a 25 bps reduction. The Fed recently cut by 25 bps in September 2025 to a range of 4.00%-4.25% and signaled a steady path of 25 bps cuts. Without any major economic shocks reported in the days immediately prior to the blackout period, and major banks like Wells Fargo publishing previews that confirm a 25 bps cut is fully expected, there is little to no room for a surprise hold or 50 bps cut. The Fed heavily avoids surprising the market when consensus is this strongly baked in. I leave a 4% chance for an unprecedented eleventh-hour shock or data error.
